# primejob

> CLI that runs a local `uv run python train.py` on a remote Prime Intellect GPU pod with reusable dataset storage. The user keeps their local UX; the training executes on rented H100/H200/A100/B200/RTX4090 hardware.

primejob is a Python CLI (entry point: `primejob`, installed from `git+https://github.com/AleksanderObuchowski/primejob.git`; PyPI is not published yet). It is configured per-project in `pyproject.toml` under `[tool.primejob]`. Common keys: `dataset_disk`, `forward_env`, `default_gpu`, `default_country`, `default_disk_size`, `include` (renamed from `bundle_paths`, the old name is still accepted with a one-time deprecation hint), `uv_extras`, `uv_groups`, `uv_all_extras`, `download_outputs`, `download_include`, `download_exclude`, `ssh_max_wait`, `ssh_retry_delay`, `ssh_auth_timeout`, `exclude_providers`. Authentication uses `PRIME_API_KEY` from `.env` in cwd or `~/.prime/config.json` (written by `prime login`). **`primejob login`** runs `prime login` when needed, configures `ssh_key_path`, registers your public SSH key with Prime via the HTTP API when missing (optional `--yes`, `--smoke-test`), then runs **`primejob doctor`**. **`primejob login --smoke-test`** provisions a cheap CPU pod, waits until **`installation_progress` is 100%** when reported, prints live pod/install progress, applies the same short post-ready pause as **`primejob run`**, then **`wait_for_ssh_connect`** validates SSH before terminating the pod. SSH uses the private key from `~/.prime/config.json` (`ssh_key_path`, set automatically by login or via `prime config set-ssh-key-path`) or `PRIME_SSH_KEY_PATH`.

**SSH / primary key:** Pods receive your account **primary** SSH public key. **`create_pod`** sends **`sshKeyId`** for the locally registered key when creating pods. **`primejob doctor`** reports registration and primary status. If **`SSH [auth_propagation]`** persists past ~60s, Prime may not have injected the key into that provider — use **`--skip-provider`**, **`exclude_providers`** in pyproject, or a different **`--country`**.

**Pod readiness:** `wait_for_running` (pods backend) requires RUNNING state, an `ssh_connection`, and **`installation_progress >= 100`** when the field exists — avoids SSH during cloud-init. **`primejob run`** then sleeps briefly (`SSH_POST_READY_SLEEP_S` in `run.py`) before counting SSH retry budgets so auth propagation time is not wasted during that pause.

**Packaging:** primejob does **not** upload the working directory. For each `primejob run` (and `primejob package`) it builds a `PackagePlan` from three sources: (1) AST import closure starting at the entrypoint, walking local imports recursively (third-party + stdlib skipped — they come from `uv sync` on the pod); (2) always-include manifest (`pyproject.toml`, `uv.lock`, `.python-version`, top-level `README*` / `LICENSE*`); (3) explicit `include` patterns from CLI (`--include` / `-i`) and `[tool.primejob].include` (globs, directory shorthand, `**`). A `DEFAULT_EXCLUDES` safety belt prunes `.git/`, `.venv/`, `.uv-cache/`, `node_modules/`, `__pycache__/`, `outputs/`, `.env`. Dynamic imports (`importlib.import_module`, `__import__`) are detected; in TTY mode the user is asked once to add them to `include`; in `--yes` they become a warning. Tarballs > 100 MB log the five largest files. `primejob package <script> --dry-run` previews the plan; without `--dry-run` it writes `./primejob-package.tar.gz` (or `-o path`).

**Pod lifecycle:** `primejob run` owns the pod for the lifetime of the command. A detached local watchdog terminates the pod if the foreground process dies abruptly (`kill -9`, terminal closed, parent harness exit). Graceful exit, abrupt local death, and remote-only stalls are covered. Host crashes that kill both CLI and watchdog need `primejob runs reconcile` afterwards. `primejob runs list --check-remote` flags runs that may still be billing; `primejob runs reconcile --terminate-stale` cleans them up.

When helping a user with primejob, prefer the SDK / CLI surface over guessing — Prime Intellect's public docs lag behind the actual `prime_cli.api` Python SDK. Always run `primejob doctor` first to confirm auth, SSH key registration, paramiko, the SDK, and the `prime` CLI are wired up. GPU short aliases (`H100`, `H200`, `A100`, `B200`, `RTX4090`, `CPU`) resolve to full Prime SKUs (`H100_80GB`, `H200_141GB`, ...); `primejob gpus list` prints the full table.

## Core commands

- `primejob login` — Prime auth (`prime login` when needed), SSH key selection + `ssh_key_path`, upload key through Prime API if missing (`--yes`, `--smoke-test`), then `primejob doctor`; smoke-test waits for install completion + SSH then deletes the pod
- `primejob doctor` — verify auth, SSH key (local + registered + primary in Prime), paramiko, SDK, prime CLI
- `primejob dataset push ./data` — upload dataset to the persistent disk (one-time, reused by every run)
- `primejob dataset list` — list dataset contents on the persistent disk
- `primejob run <script.py> --gpu H100 --yes` — pick cheapest matching offering; wait until pod reports RUNNING, SSH endpoint present, and `installation_progress` 100% when available; short pause before first SSH; then `wait_for_ssh_connect` before upload/exec; upload AST-closure src package, `uv sync` + `uv run python <script>`, stream output, download `outputs/`, terminate pod
- `primejob run <script.py> --gpu H100 -- --epochs 10 --lr 3e-4` — everything after `--` is forwarded to the user's script
- `primejob run <script.py> --data-mode attach|stage|none|local` — attach (default) mounts persistent disk; stage copies via helper pod; none skips disks; local bundles `--include` paths into src
- `primejob run <script.py> --include PATTERN` — extra files/globs to ship (repeatable; also `[tool.primejob].include`). `--include-data` is a deprecated alias.
- `primejob run <script.py> --extra NAME` / `--group NAME` / `--all-extras` — passed through to `uv sync`/`uv run` (also `uv_extras`, `uv_groups`, `uv_all_extras` in pyproject)
- `primejob run <script.py> --download-include GLOB` / `--download-exclude GLOB` / `--no-download` — filter or skip the `outputs/` download
- `primejob run <script.py> --setup-ssh` — auto-configure/register SSH key before provisioning (non-interactive)
- `primejob run <script.py> --skip-provider PROVIDER` — exclude provider when picking cheapest offering (repeatable; also `exclude_providers` in pyproject)
- `primejob run <script.py> --plain` — force plain streaming (default in non-TTY); `--exit-on-finish` skips the TUI summary screen
- `primejob package <script.py> [--dry-run] [-o path.tar.gz] [-i PATTERN]` — preview or write the upload tarball without provisioning a pod
- `primejob runs list [--check-remote]` — list local runs; `--check-remote` flags pods that may still be billing
- `primejob runs reconcile [--terminate-stale]` — detect stale local "running" manifests; `--terminate-stale` also stops billing on those pods
- `primejob status <run_id>` / `primejob logs <run_id>` — inspect a past run
- `primejob attach <run_id>` — re-open the Textual dashboard (view-only) on a running job
- `primejob terminate <run_id>` — kill a wedged run
- `primejob gpus list` — full GPU type table

## Runtime contract for user scripts

- The dataset path is exposed via the `PRIMEJOB_DATASET_PATH` environment variable in `attach`, `stage`, and `local` modes — scripts should read inputs from there. Omitted in `none` mode.
- Anything the script writes under `outputs/` (relative to project root on the pod) is downloaded back to `./outputs/<run_id>/` on the local machine after the run. Filter with `download_include` / `download_exclude` (pyproject) or `--download-include` / `--download-exclude` / `--no-download` (CLI).
- `forward_env` keys from `[tool.primejob]` are forwarded into the pod environment (typically `HF_TOKEN`, `WANDB_API_KEY`).
- Per-run state lives in `~/.primejob/runs/<run_id>/` (`manifest.json`, `log.txt`).

## Documentation

- [README](https://github.com/Aleksanderobuchowski/primejob/blob/main/README.md) — quick start, configuration reference, dataset modes, packaging, pod lifecycle, SSH troubleshooting, dashboard keybindings, GPU aliases, state layout
- [README (local copy)](README.md) — same content, served alongside the source tree
- [pyproject.toml](pyproject.toml) — package metadata, dependencies, CLI entry point

## Examples

Each example is a self-contained project (own `pyproject.toml`, `.python-version`, `uv.lock`, `README.md`):

- [examples/hf-sentiment-json](examples/hf-sentiment-json/) — Hugging Face `Trainer` sentiment classification from `data/train.json`
- [examples/image-folder-torch](examples/image-folder-torch/) — Torch image-folder classification from `data/image_folder/<class>/*.ppm`
- [examples/unsloth-sft](examples/unsloth-sft/) — Unsloth + TRL SFT on a tiny in-memory instruction dataset
